Black Finish on Exterior Door Handles

items.name: Engine
items.description: Defines the engine’s size, layout, and supporting systems that affect performance, efficiency, and reliability.
items.tip: Engine configuration and displacement strongly influence power delivery and fuel efficiency.

items.name: Engine Type
items.description: Defines the engine technology, such as petrol, diesel, hybrid, or electric.
items.tip: Choose the engine type based on driving needs and fuel preference.

items.name: Turbocharger/ Supercharger
items.description: Uses either exhaust gases (turbocharger) or engine power (supercharger) to boost engine performance.
items.tip: Boost systems improve acceleration without increasing engine size.

items.name: Fuel Type
items.description: Indicates whether the vehicle uses petrol, diesel, CNG, electric, or hybrid power.
items.tip: Select fuel type based on running cost and usage pattern.

items.name: Max Power (bhp@rpm)
items.description: Shows the maximum power the engine generates and the engine speed at which it is achieved.
items.tip: Higher power improves acceleration and performance.

items.name: Max Torque (Nm@rpm)
items.description: Shows the maximum torque output and the engine speed range where it is available.
items.tip: Higher torque improves pulling power and low-speed performance.

items.name: Alternate Fuel
items.description: Allows the vehicle to run on an alternative fuel such as CNG or electric mode.
items.tip: Helps reduce running costs and fuel dependency.

items.name: Emission Standard
items.description: Set by the Indian Government, this governs the amount of air pollutants released by cars to make the atmosphere safer for humans.
items.tip: Higher standards help reduce environmental impact.

items.name: Mileage
items.description: Shows how far the vehicle can travel per unit of fuel under different conditions.
items.tip: Actual mileage may vary based on driving style and road conditions.

items.name: Idle Start/Stop
items.description: Shuts off the engine at traffic stops and restarts it when driving resumes to save fuel.
items.tip: Helps improve fuel efficiency in city driving.

items.name: Drivetrain
items.description: Cars come with different drivetrain configurations depending on the segment.
items.tip: Front-wheel drive (FWD) is most common in mainstream cars while expensive cars or SUVs come with rear-wheel drive (RWD) or all-wheel drive (AWD).

items.name: Transmission
items.description: Type of transmission used to transfer power from the engine to the wheels
items.tip: A manually operated transmission is the most popular type, thanks to its simplicity and low cost. Varied types of automatic transmissions are also available.

items.name: Length *Width *Height
items.description: Shows the vehicle’s length, width, and height, indicating its size and road presence.
items.tip: Shows the vehicle’s length, width, and height, indicating its size and road presence.

items.name: Wheelbase
items.description: The space between the center of the front and rear wheels.
items.tip: The longer the wheelbase, the more space there is inside the cabin.

items.name: Kerb Weight
items.description: The total weight of the vehicle with all the standard equipment and all the required fluids.
items.tip: A lightweight car will always be more efficient and easier to manoeuvre whereas a heavy car would give you a sense of solidity while driving.

items.name: Seating Capacity
items.description: Defines how many occupants the vehicle is designed to carry comfortably.
items.tip: Choose seating capacity based on family and usage needs.

items.name: Doors
items.description: The number of doors define the category of car. For example – four door means sedan, two-door means coupe while five-doors usually refer to a hatchback, MPV or an SUV.

items.name: Bootspace
items.description: Indicates the luggage capacity of the vehicle under different seat configurations.
items.tip: Folding seats increases usable boot space for larger items.

items.name: Fuel Tank Capacity
items.description: The official volume of the fuel tank of a car, usually denoted in litres.
items.tip: If a car has a large fuel tank, it can cover long distances without refuelling.

items.name: Front Suspension
items.description: Absorbs road shocks and helps maintain steering control and comfort.
items.tip: A well-tuned front suspension improves handling and ride quality.
values: Double wishbone with shock absorber and coil spring
items.itemValue: Double wishbone with shock absorber and coil spring

items.name: Rear Suspension
items.description: Supports vehicle load and improves ride stability and comfort.
items.tip: Good rear suspension helps maintain balance on rough roads.
values: Rear Twist Beam with coil spring
items.itemValue: Rear Twist Beam with coil spring

items.name: Steering
items.description: Allows the driver to steer the vehicle smoothly and accurately.
items.tip: Allows the driver to steer the vehicle smoothly and accurately.

items.name: Tyres
items.description: Defines how the tyres are built and what type they are, influencing ride quality, handling, and usage.
items.tip: Choosing the right tyre type improves safety and driving comfort.

items.name: Spare Wheel & Tyre
items.description: Includes details of the spare wheel size, tyre dimensions, and storage position.
items.tip: A full-size spare offers better usability than a space-saver.

items.name: Minimum Turning Radius
items.description: The official kerb-to-kerb minimum radius a car takes to complete a 180-degree turn.
items.tip: Shorter the turning radius, the lesser space you need to make a tight turn or to take a U-turn.

items.name: Front Tyres
items.description: Defines the size and proportions of the front tyres, affecting grip, comfort, and handling.
items.tip: Correct tyre size helps maintain ride quality and steering performance.

items.name: Rear Tyres
items.description: Defines the size and proportions of the rear tyres, affecting traction, comfort, and stability.
items.tip: Proper rear tyre size helps maintain balance and ride comfort.

items.name: Wheels
items.description: Holds the tyres and connects them to the suspension and drivetrain.
items.tip: Alloy wheels are lighter and improve handling and appearance.

items.name: Sunroof
items.description: A roof-mounted glass panel that can be fixed or openable
items.tip: Panoramic sunroofs make the cabin feel more spacious.

items.name: Spoiler
items.description: Rear or roof-mounted spoiler.
items.tip: Adds sporty styling and slight aero benefit.

items.name: Roof Rails
items.description: A set of bars fastened to a car's roof to carry items such as carriers/containers, bicycles and skis
items.tip: Functional rails carry load; decorative rails are for style.

items.name: Body-coloured Bumpers
items.description: Bumpers matching vehicle colour
items.tip: Having parking sensors will save your bumper paint if it brushes by obstacles

items.name: Rub-strips
items.description: A strip of rubber fitted to the sides of the car's doors or bumpers to prevent dents and dings
items.tip: Opt for quality strips because the cheaper ones tend to come off too soon/look shabby.

items.name: Body Kit
items.description: Functional or purely aesthetic parts added to the car's body such as side skirts and roof/bonnet scoops
items.tip: Body-coloured kits look more premium.

items.name: Antenna
items.description: Helps the vehicle maintain communication for audio, GPS, and connected features.
items.tip: Roof-mounted and shark fin designs offer better signal reception and a cleaner look.

items.name: Airbags
items.description: Safety cushions that inflate during a collision to reduce injury and impact to occupants.
items.tip: More airbags mean wider coverage and greater protection for all passengers.

items.name: Tyre Pressure Monitoring System
items.description: Monitors tyre pressure in real time and warns the driver if any tyre is under-inflated.
items.tip: Helps prevent punctures, improves fuel efficiency, and keeps the car stable at high speeds.

items.name: Second Row Middle Seat Safety
items.description: Middle-seat specific safety.
items.tip: Prefer cars with 3-point middle belt.

items.name: Child Seat Anchor Points
items.description: Anchor points or strap systems built into car seats to keep child seats in place, especially during a crash
items.tip: ISOFIX is an international standard for child seat anchor points, but not all car manufacturers follow this standard

items.name: Seat Belt Warning
items.description: Mandatory fitment in cars sold in India, emits loud beeps when it detects that occupants are not wearing their seatbelts.
items.tip: Seat belt warning is mandatory for front-seat occupants, but it is recommended that all occupants wear seat belts.

items.name: Air Conditioner
items.description: System that cools and regulates cabin temperature.
items.tip: Multi-zone AC gives separate temperature settings for passengers.
values: Manual (Front AC: Fan speed control, Second row AC: Fan speed control with Vents on Roof, Third row AC: Vents on Roof)
items.itemValue: Manual (Front AC: Fan speed control, Second row AC: Fan speed control with Vents on Roof, Third row AC: Vents on Roof)

items.name: Heater
items.description: This feature allows warm air to pass through the air-con vents for heating the cabin
items.tip: Useful for defogging windshields in winter.

items.name: Cruise Control
items.description: A system that automatically controls the speed of the car

items.name: Parking Assist
items.description: A feature that aids drivers park with ease and greater precision using sensors/cameras
items.tip: It comes as a boon for drivers who are not used to parking in tight spots

items.name: Parking Sensors
items.description: Alerts the driver with beeps or warnings when objects are close to the vehicle.
items.tip: Helps prevent scratches and minor parking damage.

items.name: Steering Adjustment
items.description: A function where the steering wheel moves up/down, in/out as per the driver's requirement
items.tip: When both rake and reach adjustments are incorporated, it makes for a tailormade driving position

items.name: Keyless Start/ Button Start
items.description: When fitted, this system allows the car to be switched on without removing the key from the driver’s pocket or vicinity.
items.tip: Keyless entry and start/stop (KESS) systems in some cars also include operation via a smartphone.

items.name: Vanity Mirrors on Sun Visors
items.description: Small visor-mounted mirrors for daily use.
items.tip: Versions with light help in low-light conditions.

items.name: Headlight and Ignition on Reminder
items.description: An alert that warns one from leaving the car with the headlight and ignition switched on
items.tip: Prevents battery drain.

items.name: Charging Ports
items.description: Includes different types of power and USB ports to charge electronic devices.
items.tip: USB Type-C ports offer faster charging for modern devices.

items.name: Cabin Boot Access
items.description: Allows passengers to reach the luggage area without opening the tailgate.
items.tip: Useful for quick access while seated inside.

items.name: Headlights
items.description: Provides visibility at night or in low-light conditions.
items.tip: LED and projector types offer brighter, clearer vision.

items.name: Cornering Headlights
items.description: These lights turn left and right based on steering inputs to illuminate the sides of the car
items.tip: Enhances safety on winding roads and sharp turns.

items.name: Headlight Height Adjuster
items.description: Allows adjustment to the height of the headlight beams via a switch on the dashboard
items.tip: Adjustable headlight beam level.

items.name: Automatic Headlamps
items.description: Such headlights automatically switch on and off when they sense bright or dark driving conditions
items.tip: Useful when entering tunnels or during sudden rain.

items.name: Taillights
items.tip: LED taillights improve safety.

items.name: Daytime Running Lights (DRL)
items.description: Lights that automatically switch on during the day for increased visibility
items.tip: LED DRLs are brighter and look premium.

items.name: Fog Lights
items.description: A type of lamp that improves driver visibility while driving through fog
items.tip: LED fog lamps offer best clarity.

items.name: Follow Me Home Headlamps
items.description: The headlamps remain lit for sometime when the car is locked/unlocked to aid user visibility in dark surroundings
items.tip: Useful in dark parking areas.

items.name: Interior Lamps
items.description: Cabin lighting options.
items.tip: LED lamps consume less power and shine brighter.

items.name: Puddle Lamps
items.description: Under-mirror door area lighting.
items.tip: Helps avoid stepping in puddles or obstacles at night.

items.name: Brakes
items.description: Uses braking components on the front and rear wheels to provide safe and controlled stopping power.
items.tip: Uses braking components on the front and rear wheels to provide safe and controlled stopping power.

items.name: Anti-Lock Braking System (ABS)
items.description: An electronic system that prevents the tyres from locking and skidding in emergency braking situations by pulsing the brakes (quickly releasing and reapplying the brakes)
items.tip: ABS is a great accident prevention technology, allowing drivers to steer while braking hard

items.name: Brake Assist (BA)
items.description: A system that increases brake pressure to help the car stop quicker
items.tip: Even when emergency braking, it is observed that drivers don’t apply maximum brake pressure through the pedal, the BA system provides additional pressure to help stop the car quicker

items.name: Electronic Brake-force Distribution (EBD)
items.description: An electronic system that redirects braking forces among the four brakes to stop the car as quickly and stably as possible
items.tip: Provides extra braking force in emergencies.

items.name: Electronic Stability Program (ESP)
items.description: System designed to improve car stability and control, especially when the car is accelerating.
items.tip: ESP or ESC cannot increase traction but rather improve control or help regain control in slippery conditions.

items.name: Traction Control System (TC/TCS)
items.description: This system cuts power to those wheels that are spinning without grip/traction
items.tip: Given the option, keep traction control on all the time.

items.name: Hill Hold Control
items.description: A feature that prevents the car from rolling backwards when stopped on a slope
items.tip: Holds brakes briefly while moving off uphill.

items.name: Hill Descent Control
items.description: Automatically controls braking on steep descents.

items.name: Limited Slip Differential
items.description: This function prevents wheelspin and maximises traction by shuffling torque between wheels
items.tip: It is also a nifty safety feature since it offers more control over a vehicle's power delivery

items.name: Differential Lock
items.description: Locking differentials split power/torque evenly among both tyres on an axle.
items.tip: Useful in extreme off-road conditions.

items.name: Four-Wheel-Drive
items.description: A system that sends the car's power to all the four wheels at the same time
items.tip: Improves grip on rough, wet, or slippery roads.

items.name: Engine Immobiliser
items.description: A security device that prevents the engine from being started unless the key is present
items.tip: App-based version adds remote lock control.

items.name: Central Locking
items.description: This feature let's one unlock all the doors remotely or with a key
items.tip: Improves convenience and vehicle security.

items.name: Child Safety Lock
items.description: Such locks are built into the rear doors to prevent rear seat occupants from opening the doors
items.tip: Always activate when kids sit in the back.

items.name: Speed Sensing Door Lock
items.description: Locks all doors after the vehicle reaches a certain speed.
items.tip: Locks all doors after the vehicle reaches a certain speed

items.name: Exterior Door
items.description: Defines the design and finish of the exterior door handles.
items.tip: Flush or body-coloured handles enhance styling and aerodynamics.

items.name: Power Windows
items.description: Allows windows to be controlled using switches, keys, or voice commands.
items.tip: One-touch and remote features improve convenience and safety.

items.name: Window Sunshade
items.description: Reduces heat and glare inside the cabin by covering the window.
items.tip: Reduces heat and glare inside the cabin by covering the window.

items.name: Door Pockets
items.description: Door storage compartments.
items.tip: Larger pockets can hold 1–2L bottles.

items.name: ORVM (Outside Rear View Mirrors)
items.description: Mounted outside the vehicle to improve visibility and driving safety.
items.tip: Well-adjusted ORVMs reduce blind spots.
values: Body-Coloured finish, Turn Indicators on ORVMs
items.itemValue: Body-Coloured finish, Turn Indicators on ORVMs

items.name: Adjustable ORVMs
items.description: Different ways of adjusting the door mirror to suit the driver's requirement
items.tip: Tremendously aids driving judgement in a variety of tight situations.

items.name: Rain-sensing Wipers
items.description: When the system detects water droplets on the windshield, it activates the wipers to improve driver visibility
items.tip: This feature can be intrusive especially while you are negotiating a tricky bend at a higher speed

items.name: Rear Wiper
items.description: Improves rear visibility during rain or dust conditions.
items.tip: Especially useful for hatchbacks and SUVs.

items.name: Boot Opener
items.description: Opening type of the rear boot lid.
items.tip: Gesture tailgates are useful when hands are full.

items.name: Rear Defogger
items.description: A feature that removes condensed water droplets from the rear windscreen to improve visibility
items.tip: Turning air recirculation off helps gain faster results.

items.name: Infotainment Screen
items.description: Includes front and rear display screens used for navigation, media, vehicle info, and passenger entertainment.
items.tip: Larger screens with higher resolution offer better clarity and usability.

items.name: Audio System
items.description: Includes speakers, amplifiers, and audio components that define sound quality and output.
items.tip: Higher speaker count and a subwoofer improve overall audio experience.

items.name: AM/FM Radio
items.description: Radio support in infotainment.
items.tip: Still useful in areas without internet.

items.name: Steering-Mounted Controls
items.description: The widely-used controls are placed on the steering wheel to ease driver usage
items.tip: Keeps hands on wheel; safer driving.

items.name: Integrated (in-dash) Music System
items.description: Factory-fitted music system.
items.tip: Integrates better with car controls.

items.name: Bluetooth Compatibility
items.description: Enables audio streaming and calls without cables.
items.tip: Provides easy and quick device pairing.

items.name: Smart Connectivity
items.description: The ability to connect to the internet and communicate with smart devices to perform various functions
items.tip: Useful for monitoring and controlling the vehicle remotely.

items.name: Voice Command
items.description: Allows the driver to operate features like calls, music, navigation, or climate using voice input.
items.tip: Helps keep hands on the steering wheel while driving.

items.name: Gesture Control
items.description: Uses sensors or cameras to recognise hand gestures for operating features like media or infotainment.
items.tip: Useful for quick control without touching the screen.

items.name: Input Methods
items.description: Shows the different sources used to play music, videos, or connect devices to the infotainment system.
items.tip: Multiple input options offer better compatibility with different devices.
values: Bluetooth Compatibility (Phone Calls & Audio Streaming), AUX Compatibility
items.itemValue: Bluetooth Compatibility (Phone Calls & Audio Streaming), AUX Compatibility

items.name: GPS Navigation System
items.description: On-board navigation system.
items.tip: Live traffic support is highly recommended.

items.name: Seat Upholstery
items.tip: Seat Upholstery: The fabric or leather finish that gives your seats their comfort, appearance, and character.

items.name: Driver Seat Adjustment
items.description: Allows changing seat position for comfort and driving posture.
items.tip: Proper adjustment improves control and reduces fatigue.
values: 8 way manually adjustable (seat: forward / back, backrest tilt: forward / back, headrest: up / down, seat height: up / down)
items.itemValue: 8 way manually adjustable (seat: forward / back, backrest tilt: forward / back, headrest: up / down, seat height: up / down)

items.name: Front Passenger Seat Adjustment
items.description: Helps passengers set a comfortable seating position.
items.tip: Improves comfort on long journeys.
values: 6 way manually adjustable (seat: forward / back, backrest tilt: forward / back, headrest: up / down)
items.itemValue: 6 way manually adjustable (seat: forward / back, backrest tilt: forward / back, headrest: up / down)

items.name: Third Row Seat Adjustment
items.description: Helps optimise seating comfort in multi-row vehicles.

items.name: Fourth Row Seat Adjustment
items.description: Allows limited adjustment for additional seating rows.
items.tip: Typically used in large people movers or buses.

items.name: Interior
items.description: Overall look and feel of the cabin.
items.tip: Well-designed interiors improve comfort and driving experience.

items.name: Headrests
items.description: The portion extending from or fixed to the seat that supports the head
items.tip: Proper adjustment improves comfort and safety.

items.name: Front Seatback Pockets
items.description: Rear seat storage pockets.
items.tip: Useful for kids in the back seat.

items.name: Driver Armrest
items.description: The armrest located between the front passengers which helps comfort the driver's arm while driving
items.tip: Reduces fatigue on long drives.

items.name: Rear Armrest
items.description: Often includes cup holders for convenience.
items.tip: Often includes cup holders for convenience.

items.name: Split Rear Seat
items.description: Sections of the rear seat are capable of being folded down separately
items.tip: This function bumps up practicality as the boot space increases when required.

items.name: Folding Rear Seat
items.description: Some rear seats have the option to be be folded to offer more practicality
items.tip: Useful for carrying large luggage.

items.name: Split Third Row Seat
items.description: Sections of the third-row seat are capable of being folded down separately
items.tip: Helpful in multi-row vehicles.

items.name: Active headrest
items.description: A safety feature that automatically moves the headrest forward during a rear collision to protect the head and neck.
items.tip: Helps reduce neck injuries and offers better comfort and safety for occupants.

items.name: Rear Passenger Seat Type
items.description: Defines seat design, cushioning, and support.
items.tip: Comfortable rear seats improve long-distance travel.

items.name: Third Row Seat Type
items.description: This row could either be a bench or a pair of jump/captain seats
items.tip: When the need arises, the last row can double up as space for luggage.

items.name: Fuel Consumption
items.description: Fuel consumption data.
items.tip: Helps adjust driving for better mileage.

items.name: Instrument Cluster Screen
items.description: Presents key data like speed, warnings, and alerts directly in front of the driver.
items.tip: Digital clusters improve readability and information clarity.

items.name: Trip Meter
items.description: Distance counter for trips.
items.tip: Reset before long trips.

items.name: Door Ajar Warning
items.description: A warning light that pops up on the instrument cluster when the doors are not shut properly
items.tip: Important for child safety.

items.name: Average Speed
items.description: The total distance traveled divided by the time taken to cover that distance
items.tip: The higher the average speed, the quicker you were on that journey/trip

items.name: Heads Up Display (HUD)
items.description: This function allows specific data like 'speed' to reflect/project on the windscreen in the driver's line-of-sight
items.tip: Helps keep eyes focused on the road.

items.name: Tachometer
items.description: An instrument that measures engine speed in revolutions-per-minute (rpm)
items.tip: Ideally, the tachometer helps a driver know when to shift gears in a manual gearbox.

items.name: Gear Indicator
items.description: Displays the current gear position (manual or automatic).
items.tip: Shows engaged gear.

items.name: Gear Shift Indicator
items.description: Shows engaged gear.
items.tip: Helps new drivers shift correctly.

items.name: Clock
items.description: Time display on cluster.
items.tip: Digital is easier to read while driving.

items.name: Cup Holders
items.description: Cup holder availability.
items.tip: More cup holders improve passenger convenience.

items.name: Cooled Glovebox
items.description: Air-cooled glove compartment.
items.tip: Great in summer.

items.name: Sunglass Holder
items.description: Overhead sunglass compartment.
items.tip: Prevents lens scratches.

items.name: Driver Armrest Storage
items.description: Armrest storage box.
items.tip: Good for wallets and keys.

items.name: Third Row Cup Holders
items.description: Third-row drink holder.
items.tip: Important for 7-seater comfort.

items.name: Vehicle Warranty
items.description: Defines how long and how far the vehicle is covered against manufacturing defects.
items.tip: Longer warranty coverage reduces ownership worries.

metaKeyWords.pageTitle: Used 2019 Mahindra Marazzo [2018-2020] M4 7 STR for sale in Ahmedabad at Rs.8,25,000
